titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Louise Glück Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff, Brooke McManus.

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 12255843.
This collection contains 9 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 654 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 16, 2025 at 23:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, notebooks, writings of others, ephemera, photographs, and artwork documenting the life and work of poet Louise Glück. Correspondence spans Glück's career and includes poets Steven Berg, Robert Pinsky, C. K. Williams, and others, as well as family correspondence. Writings include individual poems and drafts of collections including Ararat , The Wild Iris , Meadowlands , and Vita Nova . Writings of others include works by numerous writers including Steven Berg, Frank Bidart, Kathryn Davis, Robert Pinsky, Claudia Rankine, Ellen Bryant Voigt, and C. K. Williams. Artwork includes oil paintings and watercolors by Louise Glück.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 59 (videocassette): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 39, 60, 62-65: Restricted: not to be consulted before January 1, 2037.
Boxes 78 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 87-89 (student records and papers): Restricted until 2096.
Box 90 (notebooks and diary): restricted until Januiary 1, 2037.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Wylie Agency on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2008, 2017, 2022 and 2025.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Correspondence, 1950s-2005. II. Writings, 1950s-2006. III. Writings of Others, 1970s-2005. IV. Other Papers, 1950s-1990s. V. Artwork, 1958-1965. VI. Restricted Material. VII. January 2017 Acquisition, 1940-2017. VIII. October 2022 Acquisition, 1950 - 2021. IX. March 2025 Acquisition, 1929-2023.

bioghist.head: Louise Glück
bioghist.p: Louise Glück (1943-2023), American poet and essayist. She was awarded the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry in 1993 for The Wild Iris , the Bollingen Prize for Poetry in 2001 for Vita Nova , and Nobel Prize in Literature in 2020. Glück served as the Poet Laureate Consultant in Poetry to the Library of Congress, 2003-2004. She published thirteen books of poetry, two collections of essays, and a work of fiction. Glück's work is well known for its precise language use, dark tone, and insight into familial relationships and death. Over her career, she taught at Yale University, Stanford University, and Williams College.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Louise Glück Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, notebooks, writings of others, ephemera, photographs, and artwork documenting the life and work of poet Louise Glück. Correspondence spans Glück's career and includes poets Steven Berg, Robert Pinsky, C. K. Williams, and others, as well as family correspondence. Writings include individual poems and drafts of collections including Ararat , The Wild Iris , Meadowlands , Vita Nova , A Village Life , and Faithful and Virtuous Night . Writings of others include works by numerous writers including Steven Berg, Frank Bidart, Kathryn Davis, Robert Pinsky, Claudia Rankine, Ellen Bryant Voigt, and C. K. Williams. Artwork includes oil paintings and watercolors by Louise Glück.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Louise Glück Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.p: Contains typescript of "Two Trees," by Ellen Bryant Voigt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "From Rimbaud," "Halo," "Oblivion," and various poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Desire," "For Bill Nestrick (1941-96)," "For the Twentieth Century," "Heart Beat," "Legacy," "Luggage," "Music Like Dirt," "The Poem is a Veil," "The Second Hour of the Night," "Star Dust," and other poems. Also contains correspondence and interviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Watching the Spring Festival," and "You Didn't Write, You Rewrote".

Clarke, Kevin Jeffery - "The World in Front of Us"
Convisser, Julie - "The Tooth from the Shell"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "The Girl who Trod on a Loaf," "The Thin Place," "Versailles," and "The Walking Tour".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Materialism," and "Tourists."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Green Squall," and other poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Bridge of Light," "Glass Book," "Heroics of Style," "In the Surgical Theatre," and other poems.

scopecontent.p: Contains "Allegory," "Draft of a Letter," "The Hours of the Night," "Loggernaut reading series - Summer 2005," "Route," "Undiscovered Country," and other writings.

did.unittitle: Pecor, Claudia Amanda - "Tea Leaves and Dark Water"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Sitcome Verité," "American Culture and the Voice of Poetry," and "Mortal Gifts".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "At the Worcester Museum," "A Book of Odes," "A Brief Guide to the Sounds of Poetry in English," "A Dialogue Between Poetry and Grief," "Invitation to Poetry," "The Game," "First Things to Hand," "Gulf Music," "Isolt," "Jersey Rain," material regarding King David, "Knight's Prayer," "Samurai Song," various lecture material, and essays.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "The Life of David," "Machine," "Chorus," "Ode to Meaning," "Rhythm and Measure in Spaces," "Stamford Address," "Vessel," "Porch Steps," "The Want Bone," miscellaneous poems, writings, and translations.

did.unittitle: Rankine, Claudia - "Nothing in Nature is Private"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "At the Gate," and "Perfect Disappearance".

did.unittitle: Rosenberg, David - "Congregation: Contemporary Writers Read the Jewish Bible"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Archipelago," and "Bright Kingdom".

did.unittitle: Selinger, Eric - "It Meant I Loved: Louise Gluck's Ararat"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Crush," and "Litany in which Certain Things are Crossed Out".

did.unittitle: Slate, Ron - "The Incentive of the Maggot"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "The Art of Distance," annotated poems, "The Feeder," "The Forces of Plenty," "Messenger" reviews, "Song and Story," and various other works.

did.unittitle: Weiner, Joshua - "From the World's Room: Poems"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Repair".

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted: not to be consulted before January 1, 2037.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of writings, correspondence, notes, photographs, printed material, and other papers by or relating to Louise Glück, in addition to writings of others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five subseries: Writings, Correspondence, Professional and Personal Papers, Writings of Others, and Restricted Material.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (videocassette): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of typescript and autograph manuscript drafts, proofs, printed material, notes, and correspondence for poetry collections by Louise Glück including The Wild Iris , A Village Life , and Faithful and Virtuous Night . Also included are drafts and published copies of early poems by Glück in Mademoiselle and The Atlantic , as well as prose, lectures, and interviews. Most drafts contain extensive annotations or corrections by Glück, and her notes are often written on envelopes, ephemera, and the verso of letters.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or folder title, with unsorted materials filed at the end of the sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated materials: Writings can also be found in Correspondence and Professional and Personal Papers.

did.unittitle: "American Originality," transcription of address by Glück
Autograph manuscript notes on verso of "Bill Irwin Introduction"
"Early December in Croton-on-Hudson," in The Atlantic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosed with a photograph of a baby.

did.unittitle: Joudah, Fady - The Earth in the Attic
"Four Poems," in The American Poetry Review
"Fragment of a Letter Home," in Mademoiselle

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosed with the book are a group of family photographs from 1974.

did.unittitle: New American Review , with editor's note and clipping
The Triumph of Achilles , published book and review

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains correspondence with colleagues, publishers and editors, students, friends, and family of Louise Glück, including Charles Baxter, Ken Chen, Kathryn Davis, John Harbison, Wendy Lesser, J. D. McClatchy, Claudia Rankine, and C. K. Williams. Many letters to Glück are enclosed with writings or photographs. Family correspondence primarily consists of short letters and drafts of writings Glück sent to her mother Beatrice Glück. The series also contains letters to Glück regarding her appointment as the Poet Laureate Consultant in Poetry to the Library of Congress in 2003-2004.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three subheadings: General Correspondence, Family Correspondence, and Laureate Letters.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Associated materials: Correspondence can also be found in Writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unidentified correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Glück, Louise, "Letters to Goddard MFA student" [Marita Garin; includes Garin's student work]
National Book Award and reviews of Faithful and Virtuous Night
Letters from Louise Glück to Beatrice and Daniel Glück

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly sent while Louise Glück was traveling in Europe in 1966.

did.unittitle: Letters and drafts from Louise Glück to Beatrice Glück
The Wild Iris table of contents
"The Wild Iris (Mother's copy)" with review of Hugh Seidman's Collecting Evidence
With school papers by Noah Dranow (Glück's son)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from Tereze Glück to her parents, and letters and artwork from Noah Dranow and Abigail Savage to their grandmother Beatrice Glück.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes of congratulations from former students, friends, acquaintances, and colleagues, including Brenda Hillman and J. D. McClatchy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of awards, reviews, interviews, teaching materials, and other professional papers of Louise Glück; personal papers such as clippings, photographs, notebooks, and early artwork and writings; and a small amount of family papers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three subheadings: Professional Papers, Personal Papers, and Family Papers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 59 (videocassette): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: American Academy of Arts and Letters May 2015 Ceremonial Exhibition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original drafts of writings and notes Glück lent to the exhibition, as well as a draft of a speech and a program.

did.unittitle: Louise Glück reading and interview with Lewis MacAdams

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a newspaper photograph of Louise Glück with Pearl Primus, a prominent African American dancer and choreographer, in 1951, and announcements of Glück's first wedding to Charles Hertz.

did.unittitle: Hewlett-Woodmere Alumni Association Hall of Fame Induction [wooden plaque]

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Contains reviews and articles about Glück, as well as letters from publishers and editors, formerly arranged in a binder; materials were removed and rehoused in folders during processing, and the binder can be found in Box 54.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Materials formerly housed in this binder can be found in Box 53.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains works by Frank Bidart, Dana Levin, Katie Peterson, Robert Pinsky, Claudia Rankine, Spencer Reese, Max Ritvo, Mark Strand, and Peter Streckfus, among others. Some writings contain notes and feedback from Louise Glück or are enclosed with correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author, with unattributed and unidentified works filed at the end of the sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Writings of others can also be found in Series II. Correspondence.

did.unittitle: Chen, Ken - "Juvenilia" and untitled poetry manuscript

Gosmann, Uta - "Psychoanalyzing Persephone: Louise Glück's Averno "
Hopler, Jay - "Treatise on the Nature of Art" and other poems
In the Surgical Theater , with an introduction by Louise Glück
Limon, John - "Death's Following: Learning Mortality in Our Time"
Pinsky, Ellen - "Mortal Gifts: Death and Fallibility in the Analytic Encounter"
Book review of While they Slept: An Inquiry into the Murder of a Family by Kathryn Harrison
The Clerk's Tale , foreword by Louise Glück
Shultz, Margaret - Poems (with Louise Glück's CV)
The Cuckoo , foreword by Louise Glück
"The Grid: A Poem in 138 Pieces," unknown author
"The Second Hour of the Night," unknown author

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted: not to be consulted before January 1, 2037.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Wylie Agency on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This accession is arranged into five groupings: I. Writings, 1972 - 2020. II. Correspondence, 1980 - 2021. III. Professional papers, 1971 - 2020. IV. Personal papers, 1950 - 2020. V. Audiovisual material, 2005.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains notebooks, writings, journals, teaching materials, and other documentation related to the life and work of Louise Glück.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The notebooks in this series are arranged in chronological order. The remaining materials are arranged alphabetically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings, drafts, and notes related to Louise Glück's poetic works.
This series includes materials from two seperate accessions: 2023-ycal-0003 and 2023-ycal-0017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note that these materials include scanned images of archival materialsheld by Princeton Universit. Inquiries about the content and copyright of the originals of these materials should be directed to the holding institution.

did.unittitle: For the greatest benefit of mankind - Alfred Nobel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains correspondence, writings, photographs, printed material, and other papers by or relating to Louise Glück.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Wylie Agency on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2025.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This accession is arranged into five groupings and one item: American Academy of Poets, 1973-2020. Correspondence, 1952-2023. Personal and Professional Papers, 1916-2023. Teaching Papers, 1984-2019. Writings,1956-2023. Restricted Materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contain correspondence, comments, writings, notes, and photographs relating to Louise Glück and the American Academy of Poets.

scopecontent.p: Contains letters and a graphic adapation of A Sharply Worded Silence by Louise Glück.
